description: Specification for logging disruptions (Störungen), local storage format, API endpoints and DB schema for later server implementation (IONOS PHP + MySQL).

database.notes: Use UTF8MB4 charset; store timestamps in ISO 8601 (UTC) or DATETIME in server local timezone.

logs.description: Records of production runs and disruptions

columns.updated_at: TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

issues.description: Master list of issue types (optional admin-managed)

api.auth: HTTP header 'X-API-KEY' for simple API key auth (or JWT for future), use HTTPS always

endpoints.name: Get Logs
endpoints.responseSchema: Array of log objects as in DB

requestSchema.logs: array of log objects as above

entryExample.notes: lineNumber is stored but NOT displayed in the UI table; the table only shows entries for the currently selected line

behavior.störung_flow: Press 'Störung' => start stör timer; choose issue => assign issue; when pressing 'Start' (resolved) save the stör entry to local storage (see format) and continue production
behavior.sync: When online, app should POST /api/sync.php with all unsynced local_logs, mark as synced on success

security.sql: Use prepared statements (PDO) to avoid injection
security.api_keys: Store API keys in server env (.env); validate X-API-KEY header on write endpoints
security.env: Use .env to store DB credentials, don't commit secrets

deployment.notes: Host PHP scripts under /api; use PHP 8.x; set up SSL; create MySQL database and user via IONOS control panel

next_steps: Add sync endpoint and client retry logic (exponential backoff)
Add Local Logs UI (view/delete/export) for debugging and manual sync
Add server-side migration SQL and a seed for common issues per line
